Change 1: Replace:
  def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
      """
      Convert a list of triples about a celestial body into a single fluent sentence.
      The function assumes all triples refer to the same subject (the celestial body).
      """
      if not triples:
          return ""
  
      # Use the subject from the first triple (all subjects are expected to be the same)
      subject = triples[0].subject
  
      # Build a phrase for each predicate‑object pair
      phrases = [f"{t.predicate} {t.object}" for t in triples]
  
      # Join the phrases into a natural‑language list
      if len(phrases) == 1:
          body = phrases[0]
      else:
          body = ", ".join(phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + phrases[-1]
  
      # Produce the final sentence
      return f"{subject} has {body}."
with:
  def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
      """
      Convert a list of triples about a celestial body into a single fluent sentence.
      The function assumes all triples refer to the same subject (the celestial body).
      """
      if not triples:
          return ""
  
      # All triples are expected to share the same subject
      subject = triples[0].subject
  
      def phrase(t: Triple) -> str:
          p = t.predicate
          o = t.object.strip()
          # Helper to keep unit text if present
          obj = o
  
          # Mapping predicates to natural language fragments
          if p == "apoapsis":
              return f"has an apoapsis of {obj}"
          if p == "periapsis":
              return f"has a periapsis of {obj}"
          if p == "orbitalPeriod":
              return f"orbits in {obj}"
          if p == "epoch":
              return f"was recorded on {obj}"
          if p == "discoverer":
              return f"was discovered by {obj}"
          if p == "discovered":
              return f"was discovered on {obj}"
